approach: the issue's framing is too narrow: it reports herdr's worktree verbs dropping env, but the actual gap is that NO --env flag exists anywhere in the CLI — the seam and both adapters carry env natively at every tier, and only a layout template's pane can set it, which is why --env reads as welded to --layout

scope and drop policy were settled with the requester at intake: --env on open/worktree add/worktree open together, and on herdr's bind route prefix env onto --launch when a command exists, warn on stderr when it does not — reusing the existing fallback rather than inventing a second policy; refusing was declined because identical flags succeeding on tmux and failing on herdr is the backend leak the seam exists to prevent

the frozen suite does NOT block this: the one scenario pinning the drop constrains only that no env flag reaches herdr's worktree command, and the prefix rides in via submit rather than that command, so the prefix is additive and freeze self-clears — but the block-5 preamble asserting the seam is reached 'not through a CLI flag' is falsified by --env and will be amended as prose, which yields zero scenario deltas and is therefore not a narrowing

the CR's real risk is the one this project has shipped before (CR 14's pattern): the env-prefix fallback lives in the layout tier only, while worktree add --env with no --layout needs the same prefix — a behavior bound on ONE route and silently false on the other; the fallback is also live but UNFROZEN, so today nothing would catch that regression

predicted floors: no Clearance expected (additive to frozen scenarios, no narrowing planned), no Conflict; Compatibility is not expected to bind since the package is pre-1.0 and the CLI additions are new optional flags — this prediction is pre-suite-read and must be corrected at the gate if the touch set forces a narrowing

why.floor: Clearance — FIRED and pre-authorized by the requester at intake, the one floor this CR trips. Structural edit class across both files: mux 14 added / 1 modified / 0 removed, layout 2 added / 0 modified / 0 removed. The one modified scenario is the frozen bare-worktree-add, whose Given enumerated only the two flags that existed when it was frozen and so textually matched the new flag while promising the opposite outcome; its precondition was narrowed to also exclude --env, the Then clauses byte-identical. This CORRECTS the run-start leash, which predicted no Clearance before the frozen worktree block was read (combat log seq:6). A round-five judge disproved the producer's stronger claim that every alternative policy was textually blocked — silently ignoring the flag would not have contradicted the frozen Thens — so the narrowing follows from the settled implies-a-placement decision, not from inevitability; corrected to the requester rather than left standing. Compatibility: no production code changed at this gate; the CLI additions are new optional flags and the package is pre-1.0.
why.blast: medium — two behavioral nodes. mux gains the --env CLI surface, the seam's carried-or-not report, and the prefix/warn compensation (14 scenarios); layout gains the root-pane env degrade it already implemented but never pinned (2 scenarios). Scope held to the flag and its one non-native route. The issue's own framing was too narrow — it reported a herdr worktree drop, while the real gap was that no --env flag existed anywhere in the CLI though the seam carried env natively at every tier.
why.novelty: high — the CR's real finding is about the PROCESS, and it is the producer's own. This project's recurring defect — a behavior specified against one route, worded as general, silently false on the sibling — surfaced across seven judge rounds SIX times, FOUR of them introduced by the producer, and ONCE by a fix meant to prevent it (a scenario asserting the flag was 'defined once and shared', which asserts the shape of the code and no black-box subject can fail, used to justify leaving two rules pinned on one verb). Each round the producer fixed the instance the judge named, never the class: an ownership contradiction was corrected in three of four documents and a frequency claim missed in the same block twice, because the verification grep was chosen from the defect already in hand. The pattern was named at intake as the primary risk and briefed into every round, and naming it changed nothing; only an external check per instance did. Filed to the SDD tooling as a scope-independence finding — the judge re-derives its oracle cold but inherits its scope from the producer's brief, which is the producer's blind spot transcribed.
why.confidence: high — seven cold sdd-spec-judge rounds (fresh context, independent), converging: the final round returned oracle and builder PASS (the first builder pass of the mission), with a single architect blocker on one frequency word in one comment, now fixed and verified by enumerating every claim each of the four documents makes about the fallback rather than grepping for the known defect's wording. Two independent rounds re-ran the recurring-defect hunt across every axis, traced each deliberate omission back to source, and found none — the second negative is the evidence this gate turns on. The final round independently confirmed the two-node scope by searching the corpus for the subject rather than trusting the brief, and confirmed the reference node needs no edit because its README already routes conformance through mux's consumer suite. Full miss test over all 16 scenarios against nine wrong subjects, all caught. Mechanical checks green throughout: check-suite, check-spec-state, scenario-overlap zero, plan-safety zero leaks. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by derived), ratified in-session by the requester — the spec lands in the async review queue.

why.floor: none at the impl gate. The CR's one floor (Clearance, for the bare-worktree-add narrowing) fired and was pre-authorized at the spec gate, and nothing at deliver widened it: re-verified structurally against the rebased base, the edit class is still 14 added / 1 modified / 0 removed on mux (the modified being only the bare-add, Then clauses byte-identical) and 2 added / 0 modified on layout. No frozen scenario was narrowed, rewritten or deleted at deliver. Every defect the judge found was fixed in the TESTS, never by editing a scenario to fit the implementation. Compatibility: the package is pre-1.0 and the changeset is minor; the CLI gains new optional flags and the seam's worktree options gain an optional env member, so an adapter implementing neither still satisfies the contract.
why.blast: medium — the implementation is a shared env-fallback module (the prefix-or-warn rule as one function both the CLI worktree route and the layout walk call), the CLI wiring on three verbs, and env threaded through the seam. 33 new tests, all bound and passing; 587 total, verify green 7/7 on the tree rebased onto the current target tip. The rebase crossed a large pane-addressing CR that overlapped every touched file; the spec.md approval conflict was resolved per the standing model (latest CR wins the spec block, the impl block stays the last impl gate that ran) and the two test blocks unioned, verified green before continuing.
why.novelty: high — the deliver phase reproduced the CR's own theme. The cold impl-judge's mutation testing caught a vacuous verification twice: first a parsing scenario pinned on a route where the split point does not serialize differently, then — after the fix — the same scenario's open row, where the split is genuinely invariant because every native route reassembles the original string and the OS re-splits on the first equals. The resolution is that the split is observable ONLY on the shell-prefix route, so the two worktree rows carry the discrimination and the open row tests its own true oracle rather than a mutation that does not violate it. Each fix was proven by re-running the judge's own mutation. The judge also flagged, and the producer closed in-CR, an envHonored report wired on the worktree-add bind route but untested on the worktree-open sibling — the one-route-forgotten pattern, caught one layer down in test coverage rather than in behavior.
why.confidence: high — three cold sdd-impl-judge rounds (fresh context, independent, mutation-driven). The final round returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true with all 17 CR scenarios verified by oracle-appropriate checks, all three prior mutations re-run and caught (carryLaunch prefix omission → 6 failures; openExistingWorktree bind envHonored → 1; collectEnv last-equals split → exactly the 2 worktree rows, open correctly invariant), no regression, no absorption, no duplication (the old shellQuote/envPrefix confirmed deleted from layout-session and both callers converging on env-fallback), edit class re-derived against origin/main, changeset present, verify green. Verified live against herdr 0.7.4 twice: the prefix genuinely reaches the launched process (printenv wrote the value). Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash for the spec gate; the IMPL gate was RATIFIED by the requester in-session — this gate carries a human verdict.

summary: when the upstream multiplexer gains an env parameter on its worktree verbs, the prefix-or-warn compensation becomes dead code and its module, its two call sites, and the seam's carried-or-not report can be removed; contingent on the upstream request being accepted, released, and adopted here
